entry.title: Cannot deploy agent in Joule Studio (Agent creation failed)
entry.content:
I am experiencing this issue when trying to deploy a custom Joule agent to a shared environment:

Deployment of Joule skills are working fine. We have AI Units available so I am not sure what causes the issue, are there some additional configuration steps necessary to allow Joule agents the consumption of AI Units before deployment (my guess is the problem is related to AI units since the deployment of skills seems to work fine).

entry.title: How to enable Joule for the SAC Tenant Owner in S4 public cloud?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I'm looking for a solution of the problem I'm currently facing.

I went through the whole Blog and got it done to setup the connection between SAC and Joule in S4 Public Cloud.... but only for a regular user (with BI Admin and Admin role), but not for the SAC tenant Owner (who has no roles).

The first time, this was the result of reading the users, like described in step 4.5 Schedule User and Group Provisioning (optional)

Not sure what the real root cause was... I had the feeling that only users with roles are processed, so I put myself (the owner) into a Team and assigned the Admin roles to the team.

In the second try, it looks like also my user got processed:

However, in S4 public Cloud, when I try to get answer from a SAC model by Joule, I'm asked if my question is related to Ledger 0L and which Company Code ... and that get the information that Joule would not be able to access any SAC tenant.

In contrast to that, my colleague is able to get analytical answers from SAC.... so the whole setup basically works!

Did anybody experience the same issue and did find a solution?

Addition: No, I have no API Access Users in my tenant. This is a fresh TDD SAC, where only 2 Users and 1 Team exists.

Thanks a lot, Martin

entry.title: HTTP CSP error while testing Joule Skill
entry.content:
I am testing out a simple Joule skill, i created. When I goto test the Joule skill, I get the following error. I am using Google Chrome.

Initially the Testing begins and the screen shows a loading spinner

 

 

 

 

 

Then the screen shows an error

 

 

 

 

 

In the console, I am getting this specific error:

 

 

Any help would be appreciated.

Thank you

entry.title: Enabling Joule studio in SAP Partner Account
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are an SAP Partner (Silver Partner, Partner Edge Sell & Service) and have an SAP BTP Global Account (Pay-As-You-Go for SAP BTP for cloud TDD (8009282)). 

We would like to enable SAP Joule in our BTP account. We have the Cloud TDD, Joule base entitlement (8020592) license procured in our account. However, the entitlement “das-application” is not visible under Entitlements > Service Assignments.

We will greatly appreciate any help in the matter.

Thank you

entry.title: "How to Get Started with SAP Business AI on S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Looking for a Beginner's
entry.content:
Hello everyone. I would like to ask for your advice regarding the setup of SAP Business AI.

I apologize for asking with very limited knowledge, but I would be truly grateful if I could get help from this wonderful community.

■ Current Situation
- I work at an SAP Partner company, and we have SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition deployed in our own environment.
- We have a BTP subaccount, but nothing has been configured beyond SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition itself.
- Through various seminars, I learned about the great potential of SAP Business AI and would like to start by building it in our own environment.

■ Goals
① Build an environment where SAP Business AI can run in our own system
② If possible, try running a free (no additional cost) AI scenario first
(e.g., Sales Order Automatic Completion)

■ Questions
① When starting with SAP Business AI in an S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition environment, what should be the very first step?
② Could you point me to any beginner-friendly roadmaps, official documentation, or learning materials?
③ For free AI scenarios, what is the minimum required configuration on the BTP side?

I have searched through SAP Community posts and also tried asking generative AI tools, but I have not been able to find concrete steps that fit my specific environment.

If anyone with experience could share their advice, it would be a tremendous help.
Thank you very much in advance.

SAP Business AI SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Service Joule

entry.title: Joule- Microsoft Copilot Integration issues
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have enabled Joule in SuccessFactors. Now we are in the process of integrating Joule with Microsoft Copilot. We have followed the below given blog and all the steps have been completed. However we are getting the below error post the Microsoft copilot integration. Error: login.microsoftonline.com refused to connect. Please help.

Blog link: Enable Microsoft Copilot and Teams to Pass Request... - SAP Community

entry.title: Joule for S/4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

We are planning to integrate Joule with S/4HANA Private cloud.  Please clarify below questions:

Does customer have both options as below? or only option B?

A. Integrate Joule with Work Zone Site 

B. Integration Joule with S/4HANA Fiori Launchpad

 

SAP documentation ( https://help.sap.com/docs/joule/integrating-joule-with-sap/configure-destinations ) asks to create below destinations:

  1. Design time destination
  2. Runtime destination - Documentation says Authentication method= No Authentication. Is this wrong as we should have Principal Propagation for this destination ?
  3. Separate destination for retrieving SAP Card nominations - This destination has fixed nameIdFormat as tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:emailAddress
    Does this mean we have to use only email address as attribute for principal propagation?
  4. Single Sign On destination (S4HANA_PCE_SSO): What is use of this destination? Why cant we leverage Runtime destination ?
  5. RFC destination for Identity Provisioning -
    Do we need to assign any roles to users in BTP? or only replication is needed?
    If customer has different Identity Governance solution to provision users in IAS/BTP, do we need this step?

Then documentation talks about BTP Work Zone setup only up to step of exposing the content. 

Is this required only for Joule to use navigational service? Does customer need to pay for users in Work Zone?

 

Regards,

VP

entry.title: Joule Agent Test fails with an error after adding the MCP server
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community, 

We try to run Test an Agent in SAP Build Joule Studio but the moment we add the MCP server the following error is thrown:
Polling deployment status - jobId=da51d2fa-ca11-49e6-9a0f-f2b96293ed46 - FAILED - logs: [ { "message": "Failed to create agent using mass deploy content due to exception {0} (error code: Tenant Administration Worker - 6011, id: 4cffa9da-8bb1-4b41-b988-b5b63a7a21bb)", "log_id": "98b736a3-9b81-49bc-80d5-c6aa59cd2725", "correlation_id": "215a5d22-126e-450d-7364-b95680418b86", "details": [], "code": "Tenant Administration - 6011" } ]

We had followed this tutorial (Which was working perfectly fine until 1 month ago but now it is not): SAP Discovery Center Card - Create a Joule Studio Project with Joule Skills 
All the prerequisites are met, and all the necessary Entitlements and AI Units are purchased and set up correctly in the Subaccount. 
The MCP server, the Joule and SAP Build are located all in the same subaccount but still it seems it doesn't work. 
MCP server is reachable through postman, and other tools, but when we add it in Joule and try to run the test, we have the above error. 

Does anyone have encountered the same? We checked everything and we still are not able to identify the issue. 

Thank you very much in advance for your help on this

----Edited----
We still encounter the above issue, and SAP is not answering in the Ticket. They haven't even assigned a processor in the ticket yet. All the mentioned points below have been checked and verified already. Was anyone able to solve it? 

Thank you very much in advance for your help on this

entry.title: Expose Joule Studio Agent/Skills as API for UI-triggered intelligent insights (without chatbot inter
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I’m currently exploring SAP Joule Studio capabilities and wanted to understand if it’s possible to expose Joule Studio agents or skills as an API/service so they can be triggered programmatically from a UI rather than through the Joule chatbot interface.

Use Case:
In our application UI, users interact with specific components (for example, clicking a marker or icon on a screen). When the user clicks this marker, we want to:

  1. Immediately trigger a backend call.

  2. Invoke a Joule Studio Agent or Skill that generates intelligent insights based on the context/data of that UI component.

  3. Return the generated insight and display it directly in a UI card or insight panel.

So instead of the user asking a question through the chatbot prompt, the insight is automatically generated and displayed on the UI based on the user action.

What I want to confirm:

  • Is it possible to expose Joule Studio agents/skills as an API endpoint that can be invoked programmatically?

  • Can Joule-generated insights be returned as structured output suitable for embedding in a custom UI component/card?

  • Are there recommended patterns or SAP services (e.g., AI Core, AI Launchpad, CAP services, etc.) that should be used to orchestrate this?

  • If Joule Studio itself cannot be directly exposed as an API, what would be the recommended architecture to achieve this UI-driven intelligent insight pattern?

The goal is to enable context-aware insights triggered directly from UI interactions, rather than requiring users to manually prompt a chatbot.

Any guidance, architecture suggestions, or documentation references would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP Build process automation to Joule Deployment Fails
entry.content: I am trying to deploy a skill from SAP Build Process Automation to Joule, but the Integration Gateway rejects it with the error: 'Cannot use Joule formation connection, ias spba token missing in request header.' My System Landscape Formation is set up correctly, so I suspect this is a Trust Configuration or IAS user mapping issue preventing the required token from generating. Has anyone successfully resolved this specific authentication block during Joule deployments?

entry.title: S/4HANA Product Compliance capabilities using Joule
entry.content:
Hello,

Are there any Product Compliance use cases for Joule—such as DG classification or automated hazard classification?

Thank you,

Sruthi

entry.title: AI in warehousing -what you need to start with Joule
entry.content:
Today, I want to focus on a topic that is transforming user interaction across our solutions: Joule, your AI copilot. Many of you are eager to integrate this powerful assistant into your SAP S/4HANA Cloud environment, and a common question we receive is, "How do we get started?"

The idea came when we have presented the webinar “What´s New: SAP Extended Warehouse Management 2025 FPS1 Update” and Corrine Lu presented, what the new AI capabilities are for SAP EWM.
Joule has begun supporting transactional actions in EWM, starting with outbound processes. Users can now ask Joule to:

  • Create or confirm warehouse tasks
  • Set shipping readiness status (integrated with TM)
  • Post or reverse goods issues
    This is part of an ongoing rollout, with new Joule skills being released monthly.


Joule integration with the SAP EWM Outbound process

In the upcoming releases of Joule monthly schedule, we will release the full capabilities of Joule feature “Talk to your Warehouse Outbound process”, providing users with the possibility not only to retrieve data from EWM backend on Outbound Delivery Orders, but also to trigger next steps and accelerate the outbound fulfillment process. In addition, users can also navigate directly in the Joule conversation to the corresponding FIORI apps.

Get an overview on all available Joule capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition via our dedicated SAP Help – Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - highlighting transactional and informational capabilities to stay up-to-date.

Unlocking Joule: The Onboarding Pathway

Integrating Joule into your landscape is a structured process designed to connect the AI copilot securely and efficiently with your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. Based on the official onboarding guide, the journey can be understood through its prerequisites and a clear, multi-step setup.

Before You Begin: Essential Prerequisites

To ensure a smooth onboarding experience, it is essential to have the following in place first:

* An SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) global account.
* An SAP BTP subaccount provisioned in a supported region (for example, US East (VA) or Europe (Frankfurt)).
* The Cloud Foundry environment must be enabled within your subaccount.
* Necessary entitlements for both Joule and SAP Build Process Automation.
* Appropriate Administrator roles in both your BTP global and subaccounts.

Meeting these requirements beforehand will pave the way for a successful integration.


Often asked: the licenses that you need to start with Joule

Before diving into the Joule onboarding tasks, it is essential to first ensure that certain necessary prerequisites are met, both for the SAP product and for Joule itself. Let’s start by examining the SAP product prerequisites that need to be in place.

  • Valid SAP Product License: The organization must hold a license for an SAP product that supports Joule integration (such as SAP SuccessFactors or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition) hosted in one of the supported data centers. 
  • Identity Authentication Integration: The SAP product must be integrated with Identity Authentication Service (IAS). Joule leverages the existing IAS setup of the SAP product to handle user login and authentication.
  • SAP BTP Enterprise Global Account: An enterprise global account on SAP Business Technology Platform is required. Since Joule is a BTP-based application, it relies on SAP BTP services to function properly.

 

For a smooth onboarding experience, users must ensure that the following prerequisites for Joule are in place:

  • Cloud Identity Services Entitlements: The organization must have the necessary entitlements for Cloud Identity Services. Joule utilizes Identity Authentication for user login, while the Identity Provisioning service handles the provisioning of identities and their authorizations across various business applications, including SAP SuccessFactors, the Identity Authentication Service Tenant, and SAP Build Work Zone.
  • Dedicated Subaccount Creation: A subaccount must be created within the global account using the SAP BTP cockpit for provisioning Joule. While a single Joule subscription can be integrated with multiple SAP products (such as SAP SuccessFactors, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP Build Work Zone), it’s important to note that one subscription can only be integrated with one tenant of any given SAP product. This means multiple systems of the same product cannot be included within a single Joule formation. Organizations must ensure there is one IAS tenant per independent entity and operate each independent entity from its own dedicated SAP BTP subaccount.

For more details regarding the Joule specific requirements, as well as which SAP product prerequisites are needed, please read the Help Portal Page “Integrating Joule with SAP Solutions: Prerequisites”

 Your Onboarding Journey: A Step-by-Step Overview

The core onboarding process involves configuring your BTP subaccount to communicate with Joule and your target application. The official guide breaks this down into four primary stages:

1. Subscribe to Joule: The first step is to subscribe to the Joule application within your designated SAP BTP subaccount. This formally provisions the service for your use.
2. Set Up Roles and Trust: Security and proper governance are paramount. This stage involves establishing the necessary trust configuration and assigning roles to ensure that only authorized users can interact with Joule.
3. Configure Destinations: Next, you will set up a destination for SAP Build Process Automation. This is a crucial step that facilitates communication and enables Joule to execute tasks.
4. Integrate with Your Application: The final stage involves integrating Joule with your target system, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud. This makes the Joule interface available to your end-users directly within their work environment.

Once these steps are completed, Joule will be active and ready to assist your users.

What Can You Expect from Joule?

With Joule successfully onboarded, your organization can begin leveraging its powerful capabilities, which are continuously expanding. As we've highlighted in our roadmap discussions, Joule is designed to assist with:

* Informational Skills: Users can ask natural language questions about business objects, system configurations, and more, with Joule drawing information directly from the SAP Help Portal.
* Navigational Skills: Joule can guide users directly to the Fiori apps and transactions they need to perform their tasks, simplifying system navigation.
* Transactional Skills: This is an evolving area where we are making significant investments. Users can instruct Joule to perform actions, such as creating or confirming tasks, accelerating processes for urgent orders.

We at SAP are committed to delivering a harmonized and intelligent user experience. The onboarding of Joule is your next step into this exciting future. For the most detailed and up-to-date instructions, I encourage you to consult the official Onboarding Joule documentation

entry.title: SAP Joule skill throwing byte_limit_size_exception
entry.content:
Hi.

While building Joule skills that gets data from actions, we run into "byte_limit_size_exception" error at times. Which says that our response size is larger than 10000 bytes. Open search in the internet is not showing any references to this error.

We optimise the inputs and outputs as much as possible, but sometimes, its near impossible to compress outputs further.

Is this a hard limit? or is it applicable only for testing? Can this be changed?

Thank you

entry.title: Document Grounding - Error when trying to get bearer token with Bruno
entry.content:
Hello experts,
We are setting up Joule Document Grounding following the guide here:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/joule-getting-started-with-document-grounding-setup-guide-1/bc-p/14347572#M189016
Looks like everything is configured correctly but we are getting this error when trying to get the bearer token using Bruno:

any hint please?
thanks,
Marco

entry.title: /hana-ai issue in SAP Build Code
entry.content:
Hi Team

We are checking on the AI Tool Kit as part of the SAP BTP Build code for HANA Cloud, when we are using the /hana-ai agent to work on the forecast models, getting below issue, same issue for all the sample prompts. 

Please let us know if any other config needs to be done. 

Issue is: No database modules with .env found.

.env file is created in Database module and also bind the HDI key as well.  but same issue.

Thanks

Nag

entry.title: Montly credit statistic when using Joule for Consultant
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm try to using SAP Joule for Consultant but I have a question about how I know Montly Credit Statistics Using License about SAP_JOULE_PREMIUM_CONSULTANT? And how I know who uses the most and who use the least credit?

So anyone help me to answer this? Please help me to answer. Thank you so much!

entry.title: How do I make Joule context aware
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m working with SAP Build (React + UI5) and Joule Studio, and I’m trying to make Joule responses aware of user interactions in the UI.

For example, if a user clicks on a specific node/component, I want Joule to understand that context when the user interacts with it.

Is there a recommended way to pass or associate UI-driven context with a Joule Skill? Can Joule be invoked with predefined context or parameters from the UI?

Any guidance or best practices would be appreciated.

Thanks!

entry.title: Joule Studio Integration with Mapbox MCP
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are working on a scenario of integration of Joule studio with Mapbox MCP. We are trying to directly use the hosted environment created for the MCP as per the documentation:

MCP Server | API Docs | Mapbox

We have created destination in BTP for the same as below:

Added the required token as authorization as below:

Additional properties as below:

However, while trying to consume in a Joule Studio, it gives up a error as below:

Can you please advise if there is any issue with regards to configuration from my end which is missing or where the issue lies. Any helpful document with regards to this is appreciated.

Thanks

Tirthankar

entry.title: Difficulty setting up Joule in BTP trial account
entry.content:
Hi there. I'm new to SAP and trying to work out whether it can fit the use case I have in mind.

I'd like to use SAP Joule with some generic business trigger (e.g. low stock) to run a Joule Skill, one step of which will be running an action, which calls an API. I want to prove this is possible in a trial account for now.

I have set up a trial BTP account, and got as far as activating SAP Build Process Automation, and SAP Build Apps in case it is relevant, however when in the lobby, if I click create I only see 'Application', 'Automated Process', and 'Business Site'. When I created a trial directly for Build Apps I was able to see a 'Joule Agent and Skill' option (However with this account I was not able to set up destinations correctly, hence moving to the BTP trial account).

How can I enable Joule on this account so that I can create this flow where some generic event trigger a skill, calling and action, calling an API?

Thanks

entry.title: AI Unit Cost per Request for Sales Order Creation (S/4HANA Public Cloud)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I would like to confirm my understanding of AI Unit–based pricing for the following feature:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Sales order creation from unstructured data

From the Discovery Center, I see:

  • AI Units per request: 0.1
  • Price per AI Unit: EUR 7

Based on this, my understanding is:

  • Cost per request = 0.1 × 7 = EUR 0.7
  • Therefore, if I create 100 sales orders, the total cost would be:
    → 100 × 0.7 = EUR 70

Could you please confirm if this calculation is correct?

Also, I would like to clarify:

  • Does one “request” always correspond to one sales order creation?
  • Are there any additional AI Unit consumptions (e.g., document parsing, retries, or workflow steps) that should be considered?

Additionally, I checked the AI Unit price list for my TDD environment, but I could not find this specific feature listed there.
Could you please advise where I can confirm the exact AI Unit pricing for this feature in TDD?

Thanks in advance for your support!
